title: MBBS in Kyrgyzstan for Indian Students | Fees & Eligibility – Atlas Mentor
description: Study MBBS in Kyrgyzstan with Atlas Mentor — top medical universities, fees, eligibility, and the full admission process for Indian students. Free counselling and end-to-end guidance.
body:
MBBS in Kyrgyzstan

  • Home
  • Kyrgyzstan

Kyrgyzstan

Study MBBS in Kyrgyzstan for Indian Students

Kyrgyzstan is a popular choice for Indian students who want to study MBBS at a low cost without compromising on quality. The country has well-recognized medical universities that offer MBBS programs in English, making it easier for international students. With affordable tuition fees, good clinical training, and global recognition, studying MBBS in Kyrgyzstan is a great option for aspiring doctors.

Universities In Kyrgyzstan?

About Kyrgyzstan

Kyrgyzstan is a beautiful country in Central Asia, known for its mountains, rich culture, and welcoming people. It shares borders with Kazakhstan, China, Uzbekistan, and Tajikistan. The country has a strong education system, especially in the field of medicine, attracting students from all over the world. With affordable living costs, a safe environment, and globally recognized universities, Kyrgyzstan has become a popular destination for Indian students who want to study MBBS abroad.

CapitalBishkek

Travel Time From India3 hours, 24 minutes

Affordable Education

Studying MBBS in Kyrgyzstan is much cheaper compared to private medical colleges in India. Tuition fees range from $3,000 to $5,000 per year, making it a budget-friendly option for students.

Globally Recognized Universities

Medical universities in Kyrgyzstan are approved by the WHO and other international medical bodies. This means students can practice medicine in different countries, including India, after clearing the required exams.

English-Medium Teaching

Most medical universities in Kyrgyzstan offer MBBS courses in English, so Indian students can study without a language barrier. This makes learning easier and more convenient.

Eligibility

Eligibility to Study MBBS in Kyrgyzstan for Indian Students

Indian students who want to study MBBS in Kyrgyzstan must meet the following requirements:

Age Requirement

The student must be at least 17 years old at the time of admission.

Educational Qualification

Must have passed 12th grade (10+2) with at least 50% marks in Physics, Chemistry, and Biology (For reserved category: 40%).

NEET Qualification

Students must qualify for the NEET exam, which is mandatory for practicing in India after graduation.

English Proficiency

Basic understanding of English is required as the medium of instruction is English.

University Requirements

Some universities may have additional criteria, so students should check with their chosen university before applying.

Is Kyrgyzstan Safe for Indian Medical Students?

Yes, Kyrgyzstan is a safe country for Indian medical students. The country has a low crime rate, and universities take strict measures to ensure student safety. Here’s why Kyrgyzstan is a secure place for Indian students:

  • Low Crime Rate – Compared to many other countries, Kyrgyzstan has a low crime rate, making it a safe place to live and study.

  • Student-Friendly Environment – The locals are welcoming and friendly towards international students, including Indians.

  • University Security – Medical universities in Kyrgyzstan have hostels with 24/7 security, ensuring a safe living space for students.

  • Good Healthcare Facilities – In case of emergencies, students have access to quality medical care at affordable rates.

  • Strong Government Support – The Kyrgyz government ensures the safety of international students and provides a secure environment for education.

By following basic safety precautions and carrying necessary ID documents, students can have a comfortable and secure stay.

Advantages of Studying MBBS in Kyrgyzstan

Studying MBBS in Kyrgyzstan is a great choice for Indian students because it offers high-quality medical education at a low cost. With globally recognized universities, affordable fees, and good clinical training, students get the best value for their medical studies abroad.

1. Affordable Tuition Fees

MBBS in Kyrgyzstan is much cheaper than private medical colleges in India, with fees ranging from $3,000 to $5,000 per year.

2. Globally Recognized Degree

Medical universities in Kyrgyzstan are approved by WHO, NMC (India), and other medical bodies, allowing graduates to practice in different countries.

3. No Entrance Exam (Except NEET)

Admission is simple, and students don’t need to clear any additional entrance exams besides NEET.

4. English-Medium Instruction

Most universities teach in English, making it easy for Indian students to study without a language barrier.

5. High-Quality Education & Modern Infrastructure

Universities follow international medical standards, offering a well-structured MBBS curriculum.

6. Good Clinical Exposure

Students gain hands-on experience in well-equipped hospitals and clinics, preparing them for real-world medical practice.

7. Safe & Student-Friendly Environment

Kyrgyzstan is a safe country with a welcoming culture, and universities ensure student security.

8. Low Cost of Living

Accommodation, food, and other daily expenses in Kyrgyzstan are budget-friendly, making it an economical choice for students.

9. Cultural Similarities

Easy adaptation due to shared traditions with India.

10. Part-Time Work Opportunities

Some universities allow students to take up part-time jobs, helping them gain practical experience while studying.

LOAN

Education Loan for Indian Students to Study MBBS in Kyrgyzstan

Indian students who need financial support can apply for an education loan to study MBBS in Kyrgyzstan. Many banks and financial institutions in India offer loans for studying abroad.

✅ Key Points About Education Loans:

🔹 Loan Coverage – Tuition fees, hostel fees, travel expenses, and other study-related costs.

🔹 Loan Amount – Varies based on university fees, student’s academic record, and financial background.

🔹 Required Documents – NEET scorecard, university admission letter, income proof, and property/assets (for collateral loans).

🔹 Repayment – Usually starts 6-12 months after course completion or after getting a job.

✅ Who Can Apply for an Education Loan?

✔️ Indian students with confirmed admission to a recognized Kyrgyz medical university.

✔️ Must have qualified NEET (as per Indian regulations).

✔️ Parents or guardians must have a stable financial background (for secured loans).

How Can We Help?

💡 Note: At Atlas Mentor, our counselors guide students through the loan application process, helping them get the best loan options for studying MBBS in Kyrgyzstan

F. A. Q

Frequently Asked Questions on MBBS in Kyrgyzstan

If you are planning to study MBBS in Kyrgyzstan, you might have several questions in mind. Here are the most commonly asked questions and their answers to help you make an informed decision.

What is the duration of MBBS in Kyrgyzstan?

MBBS in Kyrgyzstan is a 6-year program, including 5 years of classroom learning and 1 year of internship.

Is MBBS in Kyrgyzstan recognized in India?

Yes, medical universities in Kyrgyzstan are approved by the National Medical Commission (NMC) of India and World Health Organization (WHO). After completing MBBS, students must pass the FMGE (NEXT) exam to practice in India.

What is the cost of studying MBBS in Kyrgyzstan?

Tuition fees range from $3,000 to $5,000 per year, which is much lower than private medical colleges in India. The total cost, including living expenses, is affordable for Indian students.

What are the eligibility criteria for MBBS in Kyrgyzstan?

Students must be at least 17 years old, have 50% marks in Physics, Chemistry, and Biology (40% for reserved categories) in 12th grade, and qualify for NEET.

Is Kyrgyzstan safe for Indian students?

Yes, Kyrgyzstan is a safe country with a low crime rate and student-friendly environment. Universities provide 24/7 hostel security, and locals are welcoming to international students.
